events.label: assistant
events.text:
Read the gauge: 332 microns at pump isolation (t=0), rising to 436 microns after the 10-minute standing decay test (~10.4 microns/min).

That rise is too large to be gauge noise but not conclusive on its own — it could be residual moisture outgassing (which would level off) or an active leak, likely at one of the fresh braze joints (which would keep climbing linearly). Since only two data points are available, I marked this `needs_more_info`: the system shouldn't be charged until further decay monitoring or a nitrogen leak-isolation check on the braze joints resolves whether it's moisture or a leak. Findings, recommended follow-up actions, and rationale are written to `/app/answer.json`.
